meta.name: SPIN Social Phobia Inventory
meta.description: The SPIN is a 17-item self-report scale measuring three dimensions of social anxiety: fear, avoidance, and physiological symptoms.
meta.author: Connor KM, et al. (2000)
meta.reference: Connor KM, et al. Psychometric properties of the Social Phobia Inventory (SPIN). Br J Psychiatry. 2000;176:379-386.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much the following problems have bothered you during the past week.

questions.text: I am afraid of people in authority.
I am bothered by blushing in front of people.
Parties and social events scare me.
I avoid talking to people I don't know.
Being criticized scares me a lot.
Fear of embarrassment causes me to avoid doing things or speaking to people.
Sweating in front of people causes me distress.
I avoid activities in which I am the center of attention.
I avoid having to give speeches.
I would do anything to avoid being criticized.
Heart palpitations bother me when I am around people.
I am afraid of doing things when people might be watching.
Being embarrassed or looking stupid are among my worst fears.
I avoid speaking to anyone in authority.
Trembling or shaking in front of others is distressing to me.

ranges.label: No Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your social anxiety level is within the normal range.

ranges.label: Mild Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have mild social anxiety symptoms.
suggestions: Gradually expose yourself to social situations that cause discomfort

ranges.label: Moderate Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have moderate social anxiety symptoms that may affect social functioning.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy is highly effective for social anxiety
Consider joining a social skills training group

ranges.label: Severe Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have severe social anxiety symptoms.
suggestions: Professional psychological treatment is strongly recommended
Medication may be helpful as an adjunct
Exposure therapy is a well-supported treatment approach

ranges.label: Very Severe Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have very severe social anxiety symptoms significantly affecting daily life.
suggestions: Seek help from a psychiatrist or mental health professional as soon as possible
A combined treatment approach may be most effective
Do not let social anxiety prevent you from seeking help
